title: T001: Initialize npm project with package.json
notes:
## Context
Initialize the npm project as the foundation for the azdo-cli tool. This is the very first task — everything depends on it.

## Acceptance Criteria
- package.json created with name `azdo-cli`, version `0.1.0`
- type set to `module`
- bin field: `{"azdo": "./dist/index.js"}`
- files field: `["dist"]`

## Technical Notes
- File: `package.json`

title: T017: Test global install via npm link
notes:
## Context
[US1] End-to-end test of global install via npm link.

## Acceptance Criteria
- `npm link` succeeds
- `azdo --version`, `azdo --help`, `azdo --foo` all work from outside project dir
- `npx azdo-cli --version` works without global install

## Technical Notes
- Manual verification step

title: T020: Add GitHub Release step to CI workflow
notes:
## Context
[US4] Add GitHub Release creation step for master branch only.

## Acceptance Criteria
- Creates release with version tag only on master branch
- Uses built artifacts

## Technical Notes
- File: `.github/workflows/ci.yml`

title: T003: Configure TypeScript strict mode in tsconfig.json
notes:
## Context
Configure TypeScript with strict mode for type safety. [P] Parallel with T004-T006.

## Acceptance Criteria
- strict: true, ES2022 target, NodeNext module resolution, outDir dist

## Technical Notes
- File: `tsconfig.json`

title: T024: Test azdo --foo unknown option error handling
notes:
## Context
[P] Unit test for `azdo --foo` unknown option using vitest.

## Acceptance Criteria
- Test verifies unknown option outputs error to stderr and exits 1

## Technical Notes
- File: `tests/unit/cli.test.ts`

title: T013: Implement no-arguments behavior
notes:
## Context
[US3] When azdo is invoked with no arguments, display help output.

## Acceptance Criteria
- `azdo` (no args) displays help
- Exit code 0

## Technical Notes
- File: `src/index.ts`

title: T021: Test azdo --version outputs correct version
notes:
## Context
[P] Unit test for `azdo --version` using vitest.

## Acceptance Criteria
- Test verifies --version outputs correct version string and exits 0

## Technical Notes
- File: `tests/unit/cli.test.ts`

title: T018: Create GitHub Actions CI workflow
notes:
## Context
[US4] Create the GitHub Actions CI workflow for automated builds.

## Acceptance Criteria
- Triggers on push to all branches
- Steps: checkout, setup Node.js LTS, npm ci, lint, typecheck, build

## Technical Notes
- File: `.github/workflows/ci.yml`

title: T005: Configure ESLint in eslint.config.js
notes:
## Context
Set up ESLint with TypeScript parser. [P] Parallel with T003, T004, T006.

## Acceptance Criteria
- ESLint configured with TypeScript parser, flat config format

## Technical Notes
- File: `eslint.config.js`

title: T006: Configure Prettier in .prettierrc
notes:
## Context
Configure Prettier for consistent code formatting. [P] Parallel with T003-T005.

## Acceptance Criteria
- Prettier configuration file created

## Technical Notes
- File: `.prettierrc`

title: T008: Create version utility in src/version.ts
notes:
## Context
Create the version utility that reads the version from package.json. Core building block for --version support.

## Acceptance Criteria
- Exports version string read from package.json
- Can be imported by src/index.ts

## Technical Notes
- File: `src/version.ts`

title: T004: Configure tsup bundler in tsup.config.ts
notes:
## Context
Configure tsup as the build/bundle tool. [P] Parallel with T003, T005, T006.

## Acceptance Criteria
- Entry: src/index.ts, format: esm, target: node20
- Banner with shebang: #!/usr/bin/env node
- noExternal to bundle commander.js

## Technical Notes
- File: `tsup.config.ts`

title: T002: Install dev dependencies
notes:
## Context
Install all dev dependencies needed for the CLI build toolchain.

## Acceptance Criteria
- Dev dependencies installed: typescript, commander, tsup, vitest, eslint, prettier
- package-lock.json generated

## Technical Notes
- File: `package.json`
- commander is a runtime dep but bundled by tsup

title: T025: Update .gitignore with dist/ and node_modules/
notes:
## Context
[P] Ensure build artifacts and dependencies are not committed.

## Acceptance Criteria
- .gitignore has entries for dist/, node_modules/, and build artifacts

## Technical Notes
- File: `.gitignore`

title: T022: Test azdo --help outputs usage information
notes:
## Context
[P] Unit test for `azdo --help` using vitest.

## Acceptance Criteria
- Test verifies --help outputs usage information and exits 0

## Technical Notes
- File: `tests/unit/cli.test.ts`

title: T026: Run quickstart.md validation
notes:
## Context
End-to-end validation by following the quickstart guide.

## Acceptance Criteria
- All steps in specs/001-azdo-cli-base/quickstart.md work end-to-end

## Technical Notes
- File: `specs/001-azdo-cli-base/quickstart.md`

title: T016: Verify built output has executable shebang
notes:
## Context
[US1] Verify the built output starts with the node shebang for direct execution.

## Acceptance Criteria
- dist/index.js starts with `#!/usr/bin/env node` after build

## Technical Notes
- File: `dist/index.js` (build output)

title: T012: Configure commander help output
notes:
## Context
[US3] Configure commander help output to display name, description, and options.

## Acceptance Criteria
- `azdo --help` displays usage, description, and available options
- `azdo -h` also works
- Exit code 0

## Technical Notes
- File: `src/index.ts`

title: T010: Configure commander .version() with -v short flag
notes:
## Context
[US2] Wire up commander's .version() method so `azdo --version` and `azdo -v` work.

## Acceptance Criteria
- `azdo --version` outputs version number
- `azdo -v` (short flag) also works
- Exit code 0

## Technical Notes
- File: `src/index.ts`

title: T007: Add npm scripts to package.json
notes:
## Context
Add npm scripts so the project can be built, linted, and tested.

## Acceptance Criteria
- Scripts: build, lint, typecheck, format, test
- `npm run build` works (empty entry point)

## Technical Notes
- File: `package.json` (scripts section)

title: T023: Test azdo no-args displays help
notes:
## Context
[P] Unit test for `azdo` with no arguments using vitest.

## Acceptance Criteria
- Test verifies no-args displays help and exits 0

## Technical Notes
- File: `tests/unit/cli.test.ts`

title: T011: Verify version output matches package.json
notes:
## Context
[US2] Verify the version output matches what's in package.json.

## Acceptance Criteria
- Build and run `node dist/index.js --version`
- Output is exactly `0.1.0`
- Exit code 0

## Technical Notes
- Manual verification step after T010

title: T019: Add npm publish step to CI workflow
notes:
## Context
[US4] Add npm publish step with branch-based tag strategy.

## Acceptance Criteria
- master: publish with `latest` tag
- develop: publish with `next` tag
- feature branches: publish with `dev` tag
- Pre-release version suffix from branch name and run number for non-master
- Pipeline fails with clear error if version already exists

## Technical Notes
- File: `.github/workflows/ci.yml`

title: T014: Implement unknown option error handling
notes:
## Context
[US3] Handle unknown options gracefully with error message and help.

## Acceptance Criteria
- `azdo --foo` displays error to stderr and help to stdout
- Exit code 1

## Technical Notes
- File: `src/index.ts`

title: T027: Verify CLI output matches contracts
notes:
## Context
Final contract validation to ensure CLI output matches specifications.

## Acceptance Criteria
- --version, --help, no-args, and unknown option output matches contracts/cli-interface.md

## Technical Notes
- File: `contracts/cli-interface.md`

title: T015: Verify package.json bin and files fields
notes:
## Context
[US1] Verify the package.json bin and files fields are correct for global install.

## Acceptance Criteria
- bin field maps `azdo` to `./dist/index.js`
- files field includes `["dist"]`

## Technical Notes
- File: `package.json`

title: T009: Create CLI entry point in src/index.ts
notes:
## Context
Create the main CLI entry point using commander.js. All user stories build on this.

## Acceptance Criteria
- Imports commander, creates program with name `azdo`
- Description: `Azure DevOps CLI tool`
- Wires version from src/version.ts
- `npm run build` produces dist/index.js
- `node dist/index.js` runs without errors

## Technical Notes
- File: `src/index.ts`
